description: Fix systemd/init.d service to run as the installing user instead of root. When installed via nvm as a regular user (e.g. 'ricki'), the native module (better-sqlite3) is compiled for that user's node path. Running as root causes module self-register failures.
steps: In scripts/service.js, add a new function getInstallingUser() that detects the real (non-root) user who ran the install command. When run via sudo, use process.env.SUDO_USER to get the original user. If SUDO_USER is not set (running directly as root or as the user), use os.userInfo().username. Also detect the user's group via os.userInfo().gid or process.env.SUDO_GID.

In scripts/service.js, add a function getInstallingUserHome() that resolves the home directory of the installing user. When SUDO_USER is set, look up the home directory from /etc/passwd or use process.env.SUDO_USER with path.join('/home', user). For the root user, fall back to /root.

Modify generateSystemdUnit(execPath) to accept additional parameters (or derive them internally): user, group, and working directory. Add User=, Group=, and WorkingDirectory= directives under [Service]. WorkingDirectory should be the directory containing the axonrouter installation (path.dirname(path.dirname(execPath)) for globally installed npm packages, i.e. the package root). Also add Environment=HOME=/home/<user> so node resolves ~ correctly.

Modify generateInitdScript(execPath) to run the daemon process as the installing user. Use su - <user> -c or start-stop-daemon --chuid <user> pattern to drop privileges before running node. Add appropriate HOME and USER exports in the script.
Update installService() to call getInstallingUser() and pass the user/group info to the generator functions. Log which user the service will run as.

Add a unit test in tests/unit/service-user-detection.test.ts that tests getInstallingUser() with mocked SUDO_USER env var scenarios and verifies the generated systemd unit contains User=/Group=/WorkingDirectory= directives.
acceptance_criteria: generateSystemdUnit() output contains User=, Group=, and WorkingDirectory= lines
When SUDO_USER=ricki is set, the generated unit uses User=ricki
When no SUDO_USER is set and running as non-root user 'bob', the generated unit uses User=bob
generateInitdScript() output includes privilege dropping to the installing user
Unit test passes: npm run test -- tests/unit/service-user-detection.test.ts
findings: Updated existing test in service-management.test.ts that expected raw "$NODE" "$DAEMON" invocation; now tests for su-based privilege dropping with $NODE $DAEMON. All 45 tests pass across both service test files. npm install was needed to get vitest available. Review v1 fixes applied: (1) quoted $USER and paths in su command, (2) generators accept optional userInfo param to avoid repeated getInstallingUser() calls, (4) numeric GID fallback to username as group.
